action.reasoning: Observation: In iteration 15, adding CSZScoreNorm to features and using strong regularization (reg_alpha=1.0, reg_lambda=1.0) with a low learning rate (0.01) resulted in a negative IC. Analysis: CSZScoreNorm on features removes the cross-sectional mean (market average) of each feature per day. This might destroy important market-level signals that the tree uses to condition its splits. Furthermore, the strong regularization might have caused underfitting. Decision: I will revert to the feature processing from iteration 6 (only RobustZScoreNorm and Fillna) and remove the L1/L2 regularization. I will use learning_rate=0.02 with the same tree parameters (max_depth=8, num_leaves=210) to see if a slightly slower learning rate than iteration 6 (0.05) improves generalization.
